mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates reduce present values of future corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack increases, signaling weakening consumer demand and lower corporate profit growth. This threshold breach triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand. This joint weakness reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a sharp drop in the industrial production index reflects factory output constraints. These bottlenecks force firms to cut margins or delay expansion, causing negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during documented supply shock periods, or the predictive power reverses to positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index rises above 290, inflation expectations become entrenched, forcing aggressive central bank tightening. This threshold breach triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns as discount rates spike over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 290, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending. This dual improvement drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly tracks aggregate economic output. Higher real GDP levels correlate with stronger corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as market participants price in economic expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are significantly negative when GDPC1 increases.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovery phase, rising industrial production alongside increasing retail sales indicates synchronized supply and demand expansion. This alignment supports corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The synchronized rise fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when supply constraints dominate demand growth.
